que: What is the primary purpose of the AppBar widget in Flutter?
options: To display a bottom navigation bar
To display a material design app bar at the top of the screen
To render a floating action button

que: AppBar is commonly used for which of the following?
Which AppBar parameter displays widgets at the end (right side in LTR) of the bar?
Which AppBar parameter displays a widget before the title (typically on the left)?
Which AppBar parameter sets the main widget displayed in the center (or start) of the bar?
Which AppBar parameter controls the background color of the bar?
Which AppBar parameter represents the z-coordinate (shadow depth) of the bar?
what should you use the actions parameter for?
what should you use the leading parameter for?
Which is listed as something to avoid with AppBar?
options: Overcrowding the app bar with too many actions

que: Where should you NOT use AppBar?
AppBar extends which base class?
Which interface does AppBar implement?
What is the purpose of the automaticallyImplyLeading parameter in AppBar?
options: To automatically determine and show a leading widget like a back button
To automatically add a bottom widget

que: What is the default value of automaticallyImplyLeading in AppBar?
Which parameter allows you to provide widgets such as TabBar at the bottom of the AppBar?
Which parameter can be used to place a widget behind the AppBar’s toolbar and tab bar?
Which parameter controls whether the AppBar is part of the primary top area (e.g., under the status bar)?
Which parameter controls whether the title is centered or aligned based on platform conventions?
Which AppBar property controls the default opacity of the toolbar portion?
Which AppBar property controls the default opacity of the bottom widget (like TabBar)?
Which parameters of AppBar allow customizing text appearance in the toolbar and title?
Which of the following best summarizes the AppBar widget?
options: A material design top app bar with support for title, navigation, and actions
A widget that only displays icons
